title: No canonical primitive for "atomic-next-work-unit" query — existing filter / walk-ancestors / find-references require caller composition; substrate cannot self-direct
description: The operational test of the substrate canon is the query "what SHOULD we / must we do next, atomically, given current state?". To answer canonically, the framework must compose: (a) gating open DECs related to active work — user enactment gates everything downstream; (b) blocking framework-gaps with status=identified + priority P1 referenced by active work; (c) frontier of unblocked tasks — tasks status=planned whose depends_on (closure-table edges) are all completed; (d) in-progress tasks already active; (e) sort by dependency depth + priority + return minimal frontier. Currently each ingredient exists as a separate primitive: filter-block-items handles status filters; walk-ancestors traverses dependencies (when relations.json populated); find-references surfaces cross-references. None composes the answer end-to-end. Caller must orchestrate manually. Result: substrate cannot self-direct — neither the harness-confined LLM (DEC-0014) nor an external orchestrator can ask a single canonical primitive "what's next" and receive a deterministic answer. Operational outcome of all substrate canon work is gated on this composition primitive.

evidence.reference: filterBlockItems / walkAncestors / findReferences exist as separate primitives
gatherExecutionContext composes per-unit context bundle but does NOT compute frontier or atomic-next
Roadmap status rollup exists but does not project to atomic-next-work answer
DEC-0014 harness-confined LLM cannot navigate canonically without this primitive; DEC-0024 milestone-as-anchor model presupposes it
No orchestrator script composes the atomic-next query; each invocation is a standalone primitive

impact: Operational evidence of substrate canon delivery is gated on this primitive. Without it: (a) harness-confined LLM (DEC-0014) cannot operate canonically — it must improvise queries or rely on external tooling; (b) the third orchestrator query ("what must happen before X delivered") for active work returns partial info — caller must orchestrate; (c) status reporting (HANDOFF refresh; what's-the-current-focus) requires manual composition every time; (d) milestone-as-anchor model (DEC-0024) provides aspirational anchors but no canonical frontier projection; (e) any future ceremony like /context status or /context what-next that aims to surface actionable next-work has no underlying primitive to call.
proposed_resolution: Add a composition primitive nextAtomicWork(cwd, options?) in packages/pi-context/src/ (separate file, e.g. atomic-next.ts; or extension to project-sdk.ts). Algorithm: (1) Load all open DECs with related_gaps/features intersecting active milestones/features → return as P0 frontier (user enactment required first). (2) If empty, load identified FGAPs P1 referenced by active work → return as P1 frontier. (3) If empty, walk relations.json for tasks status=planned with all upstream depends_on completed → return as P2 frontier. (4) If empty, return in-progress tasks. (5) Sort each tier by ordinal/priority/created_at. Returns FrontierBundle { p0: openDecs, p1: blockingGaps, p2: unblockedPlanned, p3: inProgress }. Cross-decision with FGAP-046 (depends_on inline arrays migration to closure-table) — frontier query operates on relations.json edges; inline arrays don't traverse. Also add: pi-registered tool 'next-atomic-work'; orchestrator script scripts/orchestrator/next-atomic-work.ts; consumed by HANDOFF auto-refresh + future ceremonies. Acceptance: a single npx tsx scripts/orchestrator/next-atomic-work.ts (or pi -p "call next-atomic-work") returns the minimal actionable frontier given current substrate state, deterministic across runs, derivable purely from substrate (no improvisation).
